implementer: Staples Energy (authorized contractor); Willdan & Synergy referenced as delivery partners
summary: No‑cost energy‑efficiency upgrades for eligible Public K‑12 customers. Program contractor audits, identifies, and installs qualifying measures.
eligibility: Only public K‑12 schools; active SDG&E non‑residential electric account; within SDG&E territory.
incentive_structure: Upgrades provided at no cost to the customer (per 2025 catalog); program-managed audit, installation, and recycling of old equipment.
key_requirements: Participating facility must be Public K‑12; measures must meet program technical specs.

program_name: Energy Efficiency Business Rebates (EEBR) – Product Catalog
summary: Catalog of non‑lighting prescriptive rebates for eligible equipment (foodservice, refrigeration, HVAC, etc.).
eligibility: Non‑residential SDG&E customers; measure‑ and site‑specific rules apply.
incentive_structure: Fixed rebates per qualifying unit; amounts vary by measure; catalog updated periodically.
key_requirements: Equipment must meet catalog specifications and qualifying lists (e.g., CAenergywise for foodservice).

notes: Older catalog PDFs indicate <200 kW customers may be routed to BES; current rules should be confirmed on latest catalog.
evidence: EEBR catalog page; historical EEBR PDF

summary: Zero‑interest financing repaid on the SDG&E bill for qualifying energy‑efficiency upgrades that also qualify for a rebate or incentive.
eligibility: Eligible non‑residential SDG&E customers; measures must qualify for a rebate/incentive; credit evaluation by SDG&E.
incentive_structure: 0% loan repaid via utility bill; amount and term subject to program guidelines.
key_requirements: Pre‑check eligibility; verify measures eligible for rebates; complete application; project must align with rebate/incentive program.
measure_catalog_link: Use EEBR/BES/CEMS eligible measures as applicable
notes: Public fact sheet/handbook PDFs exist but were access‑restricted during crawl; use current web page and application steps.

program_name: Comprehensive Energy Management Solutions (CEMS) – Large Commercial
summary: Cash incentives to offset costs for installing eligible upgrades at large commercial facilities; combines prescriptive and custom approaches.
eligibility: Large commercial SDG&E customers; project‑ and measure‑specific requirements apply.
incentive_structure: Cash incentives for eligible upgrades; incentive amounts vary by measure and project.
key_requirements: Pre‑approval typically required; equipment must meet technical specs; installation verification.
notes: Program details often align with portfolio guidance; confirm current handbook/collateral with SDG&E.
